Method-provenance block
Property: condensed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual‐descriptor for aniline; identification of the most electrophilic (largest f⁺) and most nucleophilic (largest f⁻) atomic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP in PySCF (no density-fitting; exact four-center integrals).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); single-point calculations on this fixed geometry for the N, N+1, and N–1 charge states (no geometry optimisation).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation doublet (charge +1, multiplicity 2); anion doublet (charge –1, multiplicity 2).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine backend: PySCF 2.x, invoked via chemkit_engine CLI).
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged. Integrity report: status ok, trustworthy true. Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below. No other warnings were reported. The live run log was streamed during execution to the usual chemkit out file (e.g. …/20260704-050529_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01.out) and can be inspected for full SCF details.
